reasoning: Pgm3 encodes phosphoglucomutase 3, an enzyme that generates UDP‑GlcNAc for protein N‑ and O‑GlcNAcylation. Reducing Pgm3 activity lowers cellular UDP‑GlcNAc pools, which diminishes O‑GlcNAc modification of key transcriptional regulators of adipogenesis such as PPARγ and C/EBPα. O‑GlcNAcylation of these factors enhances their transcriptional activity and promotes expression of adipocyte genes, including Fabp4 (fatty acid‑binding protein 4). Therefore, CRISPRi‑mediated knockdown of Pgm3 is expected to weaken O‑GlcNAc‑dependent activation of PPARγ/C/EBPα, leading to reduced transcription of Fabp4.
